originalTitle: Nigerian police arrest 15 after viral videos of alleged sexual assault at festival
title: Police Are Helping Keep Everyone Safe in Nigeria!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from our big continent. Today, we're going to talk about something that happened in a country called Nigeria, and how grown-ups are working hard to make sure everyone feels safe and happy.

A Big Party and Some Sad News

Imagine a big party, like a festival, with lots of music and people having fun! That's what was happening in Nigeria. Many people gathered to celebrate and enjoy themselves. But sometimes, even at fun parties, some people can do things that are not kind or safe. Sadly, at this festival, some people say that bad things happened that made others feel very unsafe and hurt.

Pictures and Videos on Phones

Many people have special phones that can take pictures and videos. These videos can sometimes be shared with many, many people very quickly. When lots of people see a video, we say it 'goes viral'. Some videos from the festival started to appear online. These videos showed some of the sad and unsafe things that happened. When people saw them, they felt very, very upset. Did you know? Nigeria is a country in West Africa. It has a very big population and many different cultures!

People Were Upset and Spoke Out

When people saw these videos, they felt angry and sad. They wanted to make sure that everyone knows it's important to be kind and respectful to each other. They used their phones and computers to talk about it. They used a special way to talk called a 'hashtag'. It's like putting a special label on a message so everyone can find it. The hashtag they used was all about saying 'stop hurting people' and 'let's be safe'. This hashtag became very popular, which means lots and lots of people were talking about it. They wanted everyone to know that doing bad things to others is never okay.

The Police Step In to Help

When grown-ups heard about what happened and saw the videos, they knew they had to help. The police are special helpers in our communities. Their job is to keep everyone safe and make sure rules are followed. So, the police in Nigeria started working very hard. They looked at the videos and listened to what people were saying. They wanted to understand exactly what happened. They asked some people to come with them to ask questions. This is called 'arresting' someone. It doesn't always mean they are bad, but it means the police need to talk to them to find out more and make sure justice is done. Did you know? Police officers wear uniforms so you can easily spot them if you need help!

Many People Are Helping

The police are working hard to make things right. They want to make sure that the people who did the bad things understand that it's wrong, and that everyone else feels safe again. It's like when you have a problem at school, and your teacher helps to fix it so everyone can learn and play happily again. The police are doing that for the whole community.

Why Being Kind is So Important

This news reminds us how important it is to be kind to everyone. Every person deserves to feel safe, respected, and happy. No one should ever make another person feel scared or hurt. When we go to parties, schools, or anywhere, we should always try to be good friends. We should share, listen, and play nicely. If someone is doing something that makes you or your friends feel bad, it's okay to speak up. Did you know? Kindness can make your brain feel happy! When you are kind, your body releases special chemicals that make you feel good.

What to Do If You See Something Unsafe

If you ever see something that makes you feel unsafe, or if someone is doing something unkind to you or a friend, it's super important to tell a trusted grown-up. This could be your mommy, daddy, a grandma, a teacher, or any grown-up you trust. They are there to help you and keep you safe. They will listen to you and know what to do. It's never your fault if someone does something bad to you or around you. And telling a grown-up is a very brave thing to do. It helps make our world safer for everyone.

Working Towards a Safer Future

So, in Nigeria, the police are helping. People are talking about kindness and safety online. Everyone wants our communities to be places where we can all grow up happy and safe. Let's all remember to be kind to each other every day. Let's look out for our friends. And if we ever need help, we know there are grown-ups ready to listen and protect us. That's all for today's news! See you next time, my friends!
summary: Nigerian police are helping to make things safe after some people did unkind things at a big party, and many people online spoke up about the importance of being kind and safe.
whyMatters: This news matters because it shows that grown-ups and police work hard to keep everyone safe, and it reminds us all how important it is to be kind and tell a trusted adult if something bad happens.

originalTitle: Kenyan ex-foreign minister arrested and accused of staging his disappearance
title: A Big Surprise in Kenya! The Story of Mr. Tuju
content:
Jambo, amazing friends and future leaders of Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share a story from our beautiful continent. Today, we're talking about something that happened in Kenya, a wonderful country with fast runners and amazing wildlife! It's a bit of a mystery, and it teaches us something very important about telling the truth.

Who is Mr. Raphael Tuju?

First, let's learn about the main person in our story. There's a man named Mr. Raphael Tuju. He used to be a very, very important person in Kenya. Imagine someone who helps the president make big decisions for the whole country, especially when talking to other countries far away. That's a bit like what a "minister" does! Mr. Tuju was a special kind of minister called a "foreign minister." This means he helped Kenya talk to friends and neighbours all around the world. So, Mr. Tuju was a well-known person, someone many people in Kenya knew about and looked up to. When someone so important is involved in a story, many ears perk up to listen! **Did you know?** Kenya is famous for its incredible safaris where you can see lions, elephants, and giraffes! It's also known for its amazing long-distance runners who win races all over the world!

The Day Mr. Tuju Went Missing

One day, some news came out that Mr. Raphael Tuju was missing! Oh no! Can you imagine if your mum or dad, or even your favourite teacher, suddenly wasn't there when they were supposed to be? Everyone would feel very worried and confused. When someone important like Mr. Tuju goes missing, it makes many people in the country feel sad and scared. News spread quickly, and people started talking and wondering what could have happened to him. It was a big worry for a lot of people in Kenya.

A Worried Country

People started to talk and share their thoughts. They wondered what on earth could have happened to Mr. Tuju. Some people thought, "Oh dear, maybe someone took him away against his will!" This is called being "abducted" or "kidnapped." It means someone takes a person without their permission, which is a very bad and scary thing to do. Everyone was hoping he would be found safe and come back home very soon. When someone important goes missing, it's not just their family who worries, but often many people in the community or even the whole country. They think about their safety and well-being. **Did you know?** When someone goes missing, the police start looking for them right away. It's their job to keep everyone safe and help find people who are lost or in trouble!

Police to the Rescue!

When someone is missing, especially an important person like Mr. Tuju, the police get very busy. The police are like super helpers in our towns and villages. They wear uniforms and drive special cars, and their job is to keep us safe, follow rules, and find people who are lost or in trouble. So, the police in Kenya started a big search for Mr. Tuju. They looked everywhere and talked to many, many people. They wanted to find him and make sure he was okay. They worked hard, day and night, checking different places and asking questions, trying to put all the puzzle pieces together. **What is a "disappearance"?** It just means someone is gone, and nobody knows where they are! It can be a bit scary and confusing for everyone involved.

The Big Surprise!

After the police had been searching for a little while, guess what happened? The police found Mr. Tuju! Everyone might have thought, "Hooray! He's safe and sound!" But then, there was a very big surprise. The police said something unexpected, something that changed the whole story. They said that Mr. Tuju wasn't really kidnapped. Instead, they think he *pretended* to be missing. Yes, he made it look like he was gone, but he wasn't really. This is what "staging his disappearance" means – making it seem like something happened when it didn't really happen at all. **Did you know?** Pretending something is true when it's not is called being dishonest. It's important to always try to be honest, even when it's tricky!

Why Did He Pretend?

Now, you might be asking, "Why would someone, especially an important person, do that? Why would they pretend to be missing?" Well, the police are still working very hard to figure out all the reasons why Mr. Tuju might have done this. Sometimes, people might pretend things for different reasons, but it's never a good idea to pretend about something so serious. When someone important pretends to be missing, it causes a lot of trouble and worry. People get very upset, the police have to spend a lot of time and important money looking for them, and everyone feels confused and a bit sad when they find out it wasn't real. **What does "accused" mean?** It means the police or other people say you did something wrong. It doesn't mean it's definitely true yet, but they believe it might be, and they need to investigate more.

What Happens Next?

Because the police believe Mr. Tuju pretended to be missing, they "arrested" him. This means they took him in to talk to him more and to understand exactly what happened. When police arrest someone, it means they think that person might have done something against the rules or the law, and they need to investigate properly. After that, someone called a judge will listen to all the facts and information from the police. The judge will then decide what is true and what should happen next. It's a bit like when your teacher finds out someone broke a rule in class, and they listen to everyone's side of the story very carefully before deciding what to do. It’s all about being fair and finding the truth. **Did you know?** Every country has laws, which are like very important rules that everyone must follow to keep things fair, safe, and organized for everyone!

Why Is This Story Important for Us?

This story teaches us a very, very important lesson. It's always, always best to tell the truth. Even if it feels hard, being honest makes everything better in the long run. When people pretend or don't tell the truth, it makes others worried, confused, and can waste a lot of important time and effort that could be used to help others who really need it. Our police work super hard to keep us safe, and they need us to be honest so they can do their jobs well and help everyone effectively. This helps build trust in our communities. **What does "honest" mean?** It means always telling the truth, even when it's difficult. Being honest helps people trust you and builds strong friendships and communities!

Lessons for Future Leaders

You, my dear friends, are the future leaders of Africa! And being a good leader means being truthful, kind, and responsible. When we are truthful, people trust us and know they can count on what we say. When we are kind, we make our communities stronger and happier. And when we are responsible, we take care of ourselves and each other, making sure we do the right thing even when no one is watching. Leaders should always set a good example for everyone else.

What Can We Learn from Mr. Tuju's Story?

1. **Always tell the truth:** It helps everyone and prevents unnecessary worry. 2. **Don't make people worry for no reason:** Think about how your actions affect others, especially your family and community. 3. **Appreciate our police:** They work hard every day to keep us safe and solve problems. 4. **Leaders should be good examples:** People look up to them, so they should always be honest and responsible. So, while this was a surprising story, it helps us remember how important it is for everyone, big or small, to be honest and thoughtful in everything we do. It’s a valuable lesson for all of us! That's all for today from your kid-friendly news reporter! Stay curious, stay kind, and remember to always shine bright! Goodbye for now!
summary: A former important leader in Kenya, Mr. Raphael Tuju, was first thought to be missing, with worries he might have been kidnapped, but police now say he made it look like he was gone and have arrested him.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us why telling the truth is always the best thing to do, especially for leaders, and how important our police are for keeping us safe and solving mysteries.

originalTitle: Ex-teacher accused of Scotland school abuse found guilty of sex attack in South Africa
title: Ex-teacher accused of Scotland school abuse found guilty of sex attack in South Africa
content: A court in Cape Town found Iain Wares guilty of indecently assaulting a former student who accused him of sexual abuse in the 1980s.

originalTitle: Israeli strike on Lebanon bridge raises fears of ground invasion
title: A Bridge, a Bang, and Big Worries!
content:
Hello, amazing kids! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you stories from our big, beautiful world. Today, we're going to talk about something happening very far away from us, in a place called Lebanon. It might sound a bit grown-up, but we'll make it super simple, like a story! Sometimes, things happen in the world that make people feel a little worried, and it's good to understand what's going on, even when we're just learning. We always hope for peace, and that everyone can be safe and happy.

What is a Bridge?

First, let's talk about bridges! Do you know what a bridge is? Imagine a long, strong path that helps you get from one side of a river or a big hole to the other side. It’s like a special road high up in the air! Bridges are super helpful. They let cars drive over water, and people walk safely. They connect places that would be hard to reach otherwise. Think about it like this: if you wanted to visit your friend across a little stream, a bridge would help you cross without getting your feet wet! Bridges help people go to school, parents go to work, and even bring yummy food from farms to shops. They help everyone connect and move around easily. So, bridges are really important for people's daily lives and for countries to trade and visit each other. Did you know? The longest bridge in the world is called the Danyang–Kunshan Grand Bridge in China! It's so long, it's like travelling across many, many towns!

Where is Lebanon and Israel?

Now, let's talk about two countries. Imagine our planet Earth has many, many houses, and each house is a country. Two of these houses, or countries, are called Lebanon and Israel. They are neighbours! They are far away from us here in Africa, but they are very important places on our map, in a part of the world called the Middle East. Just like sometimes neighbours can have little disagreements, countries can too. Most of the time, people in these countries want to live happily and peacefully, just like you and your friends want to play nicely together. But sometimes, grown-ups make choices that cause problems. It’s always better when neighbours talk and share, isn’t it? When countries talk, they can often find solutions without any trouble. Did you know? Countries often share borders, which are like invisible lines on the ground that show where one country ends and another begins! It helps everyone know where their country starts and stops.

What Happened to the Qasmiyeh Bridge?

So, in Lebanon, there is a very important bridge called the Qasmiyeh Bridge. This bridge helps many people travel and move things every single day. Sadly, something happened to this bridge recently. There was a big "strike" from Israel. What does "strike" mean? It means something like a big hit or an attack. It's like someone threw a very strong, powerful stone at the bridge, and it caused damage. Imagine your favourite toy car, and someone hits it really hard. That’s what happened, but to a giant bridge! When a strike happens, it makes a big noise and can break things into pieces. This strike hurt the Qasmiyeh Bridge quite badly. When bridges get hurt, it means people can't use them anymore to travel. This makes it harder for everyone to get around and do their daily things, like going to school or getting supplies. It can make people feel sad and worried about what might happen next. Losing a bridge is a big problem because it stops people from connecting and moving freely. Everyone relies on these important paths. Did you know? Engineers are special builders who design and fix bridges to make them super strong and safe for everyone to use! They work hard to keep our world connected.

Why Are People Worried?

Now, let's talk about feeling worried. Have you ever felt worried about something? Maybe before a big test or when your pet is sick? That's how some people in Lebanon are feeling right now. They are worried because of this big hit on their important bridge. When something like this happens, people start to think: "What if something else bad happens?" The people in Lebanon are worried that this attack on the Qasmiyeh Bridge could be a "prelude" to something bigger. "Prelude" is a fancy word for "the start of something." It means they are scared it might be the very beginning of a much bigger problem that could get even more serious. They are afraid that soldiers from Israel might come into their country, Lebanon. This is a very big worry, and it's something called a "ground invasion." No one wants soldiers from another country to come into their land without being invited. It’s a very serious thing, and it makes everyone feel unsafe and scared for their families.

What Does "Ground Invasion" Mean?

Let's talk more about "ground invasion." Imagine your house, and suddenly, people from another neighbourhood try to come into your garden and your house without asking. That’s a bit like a ground invasion for a country. It means soldiers – who are people trained to fight and protect – from one country try to walk into and take control of the land of another country. They come across the border onto another country's soil. When soldiers come onto another country's land, it can cause a lot of big problems and sadness. There can be fighting, and people can get hurt. Houses can be damaged, and families might have to leave their homes quickly to find a safe place. This is why the people in Lebanon are so worried right now. They hope that this big problem will not get even bigger and that no soldiers will try to cross into their land. They want to stay safe in their own homes and towns. Countries have rules about not going into each other's land without permission. It's like how we have rules about not taking other people's toys without asking. Everyone wants to feel safe and protected in their own homes and countries. So, when people talk about a "ground invasion," they are talking about a very serious situation that could bring a lot of trouble and sadness to many innocent people. Did you know? Many countries have special armies to protect their own land and people, but everyone hopes they don't have to fight and can live in peace instead! Talking and listening are much better than fighting.

Wishing for Peace

So, what does all this mean? It means that right now, people in Lebanon are feeling a bit scared and worried about what might happen next, after their bridge was hit. They are hoping that the problem won't get any bigger and that everyone can stay safe. It's a reminder that even far away, things are happening that affect real people, just like us, and our hearts go out to them. We all wish for peace in the world, don't we? Peace means everyone can live happily and safely, without fighting or worrying about danger. It means talking things out instead of hitting or fighting, and sharing instead of taking. It's important to remember that even when there are problems, many good people work very hard to bring peace and make sure everyone is safe and treated fairly. Let's hope that the people in Lebanon and Israel can find a way to live side-by-side peacefully, and that the worries about the Qasmiyeh Bridge can soon go away. We always want everyone in the world to be safe and happy, living in peace with their neighbours. Thank you for listening, and remember to always be kind to your neighbours, near and far! Together, we can hope for a peaceful world.
summary: People in Lebanon are worried because a bridge was hit by Israel, and they are scared that this might lead to soldiers coming into their country.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries have problems and attacks happen, it makes people scared and unsafe, and everyone wants to live in peace without fighting.

title: Which countries have strategic oil reserves – and how much?

title: Oil falls and shares rebound after Trump says talks have been held to end war
content: Energy prices fall and stock markets rebound after the US president says "very good and productive" talks have been held.

title: UN warns Earth's climate being 'pushed beyond its limits' as El Niño looms
content: The Earth's climate is further out of balance than at any time in recorded history, the UN's weather agency says.

originalTitle: HK police can now demand phone passwords under new national security rules
title: HK police can now demand phone passwords under new national security rules
content: Those who do not comply can be jailed for up to a year and fined up to HK$100,000 ($12,700; £9,600).

title: Sunderland not very good compared with Newcastle - Gordon
content: Anthony Gordon says Newcastle United's defeat by rivals Sunderland is "not good enough" because the Black Cats are "not very good" compared with the Magpies.

title: Foden final appearance like a charity sub - Rooney
content: Phil Foden's injury-time appearance in Manchester City's Carabao Cup final victory over Arsenal felt like a "charity" substitution, says former England captain Wayne Rooney.

originalTitle: Spurs offer condolences to Tudor after father's death
title: Spurs offer condolences to Tudor after father's death
content: Tottenham offer condolences to interim head coach Igor Tudor following the death of his father.
